title: Employment Insurance services in My Service Canada Account - Canada.ca
content:
## Employment Insurance services in My Service Canada Account

## Employment Insurance services in MSCA
- How to access Employment Insurance services in MSCA
- How to check information about your claims
- How to submit and access your documents online
- How to access your records of employment
- How to update your personal information
- How to get your T4E tax slips in MSCA
- EI program for self-employed people

## How to access Employment Insurance services in MSCA
To access Employment Insurance (EI) services in My Service Canada Account (MSCA), you must register or sign in to MSCA.
Once registered or signed in, go to the “Employment Insurance” section on your dashboard.
Important : To apply for EI benefits, visit Employment Insurance benefits .
Register or Sign in to MSCA

## How to check information about your claim
Once you've applied for EI benefits,  you can check information about your claims in MSCA.
On your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance benefits” and “View my status updates and messages” to check:
- your EI claim status
- details about your claim
- messages you have received
Note: This information is only available once your claim is finalized.
On your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance benefits” then “View my latest claim” to access information about your current claim for EI benefits, such as:
- the types of benefits
- the number of weeks paid
On your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance benefits” then “View my past claim” to access information about your past EI claims, including payment information. Information about past claims remains available in MSCA for at least 7 years.
We can notify you by email when new information about your EI claim is posted in MSCA.
To register for Alert Me notifications, on your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance benefits” then “Manage email notifications (Alert me).”
You won't receive more than one alert per 24-hour period.
You can unsubscribe by selecting “Manage my Alert Me profile” and “Unsubscribe from Alert Me.” You can subscribe again at any time.
Note: Subscribing or unsubscribing from Alert Me does not affect your MSCA security notifications.
If you submit your reports online or by telephone, you can access your report details by selecting “Employment Insurance  benefits” then “View my payments” on your MSCA dashboard. If you submit paper reports, this service isn't available.
You can complete your reports online directly through the Internet Reporting Service .
Payment information is updated the next business day after your report is processed. Processing occurs each evening from Sunday to Thursday. If you file your report on Friday or Saturday, your payment information will appear on Monday.
Please note that holidays can affect the processing schedule.
On your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance benefits” then “View my letters” to access and print some letters that we have mailed to you. Note that some letters you’ve received may not appear here.
On your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance benefits” then “Report a mistake” if you realize that:
- you’ve made a mistake filling out your EI report or any other form submitted for your claim
- there's a change in your circumstances that may affect your EI benefits
It's important that you notify us as soon as possible to avoid penalties or problems with your claim.

## How to submit and access your documents online
You can use the secure upload tool to electronically submit supporting documents for your EI claim.
On your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance benefits” then “Submit documents” to launch the tool.
On your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance  benefits” then "View my documents" for a list of documents that you've already submitted and the date they were submitted. If you wish, you can print a confirmation page showing this information.
On your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance  benefits” then "Submit eForms" to launch the eForms service.
If you're receiving EI benefits, you can use eForms to provide us information about:
- an absence from Canada
- a course or training program that you're attending or will be attending in the next 28 days
- other parents' information for parental benefits
If you wish, you can save a draft of your eForm and complete it later. It will be available for you to edit for 24 hours. You can only save one draft of each eForm at a time. You can also access and print eForms that you've submitted within the last 2 years.
Depending on your benefit type, certain eForms may not be displayed. If you're receiving maternity or caregiving benefits and need to provide information for your claim, you'll need to contact us .

## How to access your records of employment
On your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance  benefits” then “View my records of employment” to access and print your electronic ROEs. You can also verify information about your paper ROEs.
ROEs are available on MSCA for 7 years following the date they're issued.

## How to update  your personal information
This service is currently not available in MSCA, but there are other ways to update your personal information .
You must let us know of any changes to your mailing address or telephone number to prevent delays in receiving your benefits.
If you've moved to a new province, you must also update your province of residence .
If you've moved to a new province or territory, you must update your profile with your new information to continue accessing your EI claim information online.
On your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance benefits” then “Update my personal information” and ”Province of residence.”
This service is currently not available in MSCA, but there are other ways to update your direct deposit information .
You can change your language preference by selecting “Employment Insurance benefits” then “Update my personal information” and “Language of correspondence.”
Written communications will be sent to you in the language indicated on your file.

## How to get your T4E tax slips in MSCA
You can access, save and print your T4E slips in MSCA if you:
- received EI benefits this year or in the last 6 years
- repaid an EI overpayment from a previous tax year
To get your T4E slips in MSCA, on your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance benefits” then “View my tax slips.”
For any other tax slips (such as T3, T4, T4RIF, T4RSP, T5, T5007 or T5008), sign in to your CRA account or contact the Canada Revenue Agency .
To learn more about how and when to file your income tax return, visit Get ready to file a tax return .
Your T4E slips will not be in MSCA if:
- You have a negative benefit amount If you were overpaid and the overpayment is greater than the total benefit amount paid during the tax year, your T4E slip will not be available in MSCA. It will be mailed to you.
- If you were overpaid and the overpayment is greater than the total benefit amount paid during the tax year, your T4E slip will not be available in MSCA. It will be mailed to you.
- You have a new Social Insurance Number (SIN) If you recently obtained a new SIN, you will not be able to access any T4E slips issued under your previous SIN in MSCA. Contact us to request copies by mail.
- If you recently obtained a new SIN, you will not be able to access any T4E slips issued under your previous SIN in MSCA. Contact us to request copies by mail.
You can choose to receive your T4E slips by mail or online. To change your delivery option, on your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance  benefits,” then “Change my tax slips delivery options.”
If you chose to receive your T4E slips online:
- they’ll be available in MSCA as early as  February 1
- you won't receive copies by mail
Electronic tax slips for the current tax year are available in MSCA as early as February 1 of each year.
Paper tax slips are mailed by the end of February of each year. Ensure that your mailing address is correct in MSCA before December 31 of the previous year.
Your T4E tax slips may be amended after they're issued.
You can access, save, or print amended T4E slips in MSCA.
You can't access tax slips online services in MSCA if you're:
- a registered trustee
- another legal representative taking care of a client's financial affairs
To complete transactions on another person's behalf, contact us .

## EI program for self-employed people
On your MSCA dashboard, select “Employment Insurance benefits” then “View my agreement status (self-employed)” to:
- enter into an agreement to participate in the EI program as a self-employed person
- check the status of your agreement
- withdraw from the program
On your MSCA dashboard, select “Benefits for self-employed people: Compassionate care” and “Manage my self-employed compassionate care benefits” to:
- start or resume an application
- check the status of an application you submitted
- complete your reports
- access payment information

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
